provenance.source_corpus: Evaluating the efficacy of seasonal grazing and livestock exclusion as restoration tools for birds in riparian habitat of the Okanagan Valley, British Columbia, Canada

source_spans.text: The abundance of W. Yellow-breasted Chats significantly increased between 2002 and 2013 in areas where restoration activities occurred.
The abundance of W. Yellow-breasted Chats significantly increased between 2002 and 2013 in areas where restoration activities occurred.

nodes.name: breeding performance of W. Yellow-breasted Chats

source_spans.text: Cattle exclusion did not lead to an improvement in the breeding performance of W. Yellow-breasted Chats at restoration sites.

Restoration did not have significant effects on the abundance, richness, or breeding performance of other riparian birds at the restoration sites independent of temporal changes that occurred at reference sites.

A combination of permanent and seasonal exclusion of livestock can lead to increased abundance of riparian birds that depend on early successional habitat for nesting such as Western Yellow-breasted Chats.
